title: BACKND Base error catalog
summary: Acts as a broad troubleshooting index organized around HTTP-like status codes and BACKND-specific error names. It is useful for classification, debugging paths, and narrowing a failure to a likely cause family.
capabilities: classify failures by status code family
map an error name to a likely cause category
route troubleshooting requests to more specific feature docs
highlight that exact error messages should be matched against the official catalog
prerequisites: A concrete error response, status code, or errorCode is available
The calling context is known when interpreting the same error across different APIs
common_pitfalls: Treating one error name as globally identical across every feature without checking the API context
Overfitting to translated message text instead of using status and error code together
Using this catalog alone when the issue is actually a prerequisite failure such as missing initialization or missing credentials
public_notes: This topic intentionally stays high level because the upstream error catalog is large and exact message mappings may evolve.
Agents should quote exact error details only after verifying the current official documentation or the live response payload.
